body:
### Problem

Heap UAF possible with ui2 when using `:messages` after an `nvim_echo` with a custom `kind` set.

No time to investigate, but looks like the `kind` string is being deallocated via `arena_mem_free`? (I think `msg_ext_kind` users assume it has static lifetime?)

If so, it's technically not specific to ui2, but it's an easy way to repro.

```
=================================================================
==168700==ERROR: AddressSanitizer: heap-use-after-free on address 0x7d5e47719908 at pc 0x00000041bcdf bp 0x7ffff79432a0 sp 0x7ffff7942a68

```

### Steps to reproduce

```lua
require("vim._core.ui2").enable{enable=true}
vim.defer_fn(function()
  vim.api.nvim_echo({{'a'}}, true, {kind='foo'})
  vim.o.guicursor = '' -- pending mode update go brrr
  vim.api.nvim__redraw({flush=true}) -- ui_flush -> arena_mem_free go brrr
  vim.cmd.messages() -- oh no
end, 250)
```

### Expected behavior

No heap UAF.

### Nvim version (nvim -v)

NVIM v0.12.0-dev-2571+gd3bca3b7fa

### Vim (not Nvim) behaves the same?

N/A

### Operating system/version

Fedora 43 Workstation

### Terminal name/version

ghostty 1.3.0, tmux 3.5a

### $TERM environment variable

tmux-256color

### Installation

Sauce

comment_data.body: Does the backtrace change if you change `REUSE_MAX` from 4 to 0?

Yup, no need to trigger the free in `ui_flush` in that case: (that's useful!)


title: ui2: `:messages` heap-use-after-free after `nvim_echo` with `kind`
